cities.name: Tokyo, Japan
cities.description: A neon-lit metropolis where ancient shrines hide between skyscrapers and cherry blossoms paint the streets pink every spring.

cities.name: Kyoto, Japan
cities.description: Step into a living museum of wooden temples, geisha districts, and moss-covered zen gardens.

cities.name: Rome, Italy
cities.description: Walk through 2,000 years of history — from the Colosseum at golden hour to gelato at the Trevi Fountain.

cities.name: Venice, Italy
cities.description: A floating city of canals, masked carnivals, and Renaissance palaces slowly sinking into the Adriatic sea.

cities.name: Marrakech, Morocco
cities.description: Lose yourself in a labyrinth of souks, riads, and rooftop terraces perfumed with saffron and rose water.

cities.name: Chefchaouen, Morocco
cities.description: The Blue Pearl of Morocco — a mountain village painted entirely in shades of electric blue and cobalt.

temples.name: Angkor Wat, Cambodia
temples.description: The world's largest religious monument — a stone galaxy of towers rising from a moat at sunrise, wrapped in jungle.

temples.name: Petra, Jordan
temples.description: The Rose City carved directly into rose-red cliffs by the Nabataean civilization over 2,000 years ago.

temples.name: Borobudur, Indonesia
temples.description: A 9th-century Buddhist mandala in stone, wrapped in morning mist and surrounded by volcanic peaks.

beaches.name: Bora Bora, French Polynesia
beaches.description: Overwater bungalows hovering above a lagoon so impossibly turquoise it looks like it was Photoshopped by God.

beaches.name: Navagio Beach, Greece
beaches.description: A shipwreck rusting on blinding white sand, enclosed by 200-meter limestone cliffs — accessible only by boat.

beaches.name: Phi Phi Islands, Thailand
beaches.description: Emerald waters, karst limestone towers, and long-tail boats — the island paradise that launched a thousand screensavers.
